scenes.text: In Scene 1, we introduce Docker, a platform used to develop, ship, and run applications in containers. It allows developers to package an application with all its parts, including libraries and dependencies, and deploy it as a single package. This ensures that the application runs seamlessly regardless of the environment, thereby improving consistency and scalability. Key components include Docker images, containers, and Docker Hub.
scenes.image_prompt: graph TD; A[Docker] --> B[Docker Images]; A --> C[Containers]; A --> D[Docker Hub]; C --> E[Application Deployment]; D --> F[Image Repository];

scenes.text: Scene 2 delves into how Docker simplifies Continuous Integration and Continuous Deployment (CI/CD). By using Docker, teams can automate the building and testing of images as code changes are made, leading to more efficient collaboration and quicker deployment cycles. The scene also explores the role of Docker Compose in managing multi-container applications, providing a tool to define and run complex architectures using simple YAML files.
scenes.image_prompt: graph TD; G[CI/CD Pipeline] --> H[Build Docker Images]; H --> I[Test Containers]; I --> J[Deploy to Production]; K[Docker Compose] --> L[Multi-Container Apps]; L --> M[Defined in YAML];
